name: 6DoF robot arm module inspection fixture
intent: Enumerate the published base, small-drive, big-drive, connector, purchased, and fastener instances and check basic driver access against conservative part envelopes.

parts.name: Printed base shell
parts.purpose: Support the base bearing stack

parts.name: Printed base bearing spacer
parts.purpose: Separate the two base bearings

parts.name: Printed base rotor
parts.purpose: Transfer base-axis rotation to the next module

parts.name: Lower base bearing
parts.purpose: Lower radial support in the base stack

parts.name: Upper base bearing
parts.purpose: Upper radial support in the base stack

parts.name: Base axis retaining screw
parts.purpose: Retain the illustrated base rotor stack

fastener.basis: M2.5x20 is labeled in the source illustration; the socket drive and key size are not specified there.

parts.name: Printed small-drive body
parts.purpose: House the small servo and bearing

parts.name: Printed small-drive rotor
parts.purpose: Transfer torque from the MG90S servo

parts.name: Small-drive bearing
parts.purpose: Support the small-drive rotor

parts.name: Small-drive servo
parts.purpose: Actuate the small drive directly

parts.name: Small servo upper mounting screw
parts.purpose: Clamp the MG90S upper mounting tab

fastener.basis: M3x10 is labeled in the source illustration; the socket drive and key size are not specified there.

parts.name: Small servo lower mounting screw
parts.purpose: Clamp the MG90S lower mounting tab

fastener.basis: M3x10 is labeled in the source illustration; the socket drive and key size are not specified there.

parts.name: Small-drive rotor screw
parts.purpose: Retain the small-drive rotor

fastener.basis: M2.5x16 is labeled in the source illustration; the socket drive and key size are not specified there.

parts.name: Printed big-drive body
parts.purpose: House the large servo and bearing

parts.name: Printed big-drive rotor
parts.purpose: Transfer torque from the MG996R servo

parts.name: Big-drive bearing
parts.purpose: Support the big-drive rotor

parts.name: Big-drive servo
parts.purpose: Actuate the big drive directly

parts.name: Big servo upper mounting screw A
parts.purpose: Clamp the first upper MG996R mounting point

fastener.basis: Two M4x10 screws are labeled in the source illustration; the socket drive and key size are not specified there.

parts.name: Big servo upper mounting screw B
parts.purpose: Clamp the second upper MG996R mounting point

fastener.basis: Two M4x10 screws are labeled in the source illustration; the socket drive and key size are not specified there.

parts.name: Big servo lower mounting screw A
parts.purpose: Clamp the first lower MG996R mounting point

fastener.basis: Two M4x18 screws are labeled in the source illustration; the socket drive and key size are not specified there.

parts.name: Big servo lower mounting screw B
parts.purpose: Clamp the second lower MG996R mounting point

fastener.basis: Two M4x18 screws are labeled in the source illustration; the socket drive and key size are not specified there.

parts.name: Big-drive rotor screw
parts.purpose: Retain the big-drive rotor

fastener.basis: M3x20 is labeled in the source illustration; the socket drive and key size are not specified there.

parts.name: Printed straight connector A
parts.purpose: Connect two drive modules with a straight link

parts.name: Printed straight connector B
parts.purpose: Second physical instance of the straight link

parts.name: Printed corner connector
parts.purpose: Connect drive modules at a right angle

parts.name: Representative module joining screw
parts.purpose: Represent the mostly M3x10 fasteners joining modules

fastener.basis: The source says module connections use M3 screws of varying lengths, mostly M3x10; the drive is not specified.

tool_access_cones.name: Base axis driver cone
tool_access_cones.rationale: Approach the base retaining screw along the illustrated base axis.

tool_access_cones.name: Small servo upper driver cone
tool_access_cones.rationale: Approach the external upper servo tab from outside the module.

tool_access_cones.name: Small servo lower driver cone
tool_access_cones.rationale: Approach the external lower servo tab from outside the module.

tool_access_cones.name: Small rotor driver cone
tool_access_cones.rationale: Approach along the small rotor axis from its exposed end.

tool_access_cones.name: Big servo upper A driver cone
tool_access_cones.rationale: Approach the first external upper servo mount.

tool_access_cones.name: Big servo upper B driver cone
tool_access_cones.rationale: Approach the second external upper servo mount.

tool_access_cones.name: Big servo lower A driver cone
tool_access_cones.rationale: Approach the first external lower servo mount.

tool_access_cones.name: Big servo lower B driver cone
tool_access_cones.rationale: Approach the second external lower servo mount.

tool_access_cones.name: Big rotor driver cone
tool_access_cones.rationale: Approach along the big rotor axis from its exposed end.

tool_access_cones.name: Connector joiner driver cone
tool_access_cones.rationale: Represent an external radial approach to a module-joining screw.

assumptions: This is a source-based module inspection fixture, not a claim about the exact pictured 6DoF build BOM.
Every envelope is a simplified conservative proxy expressed in millimetres, not a bound extracted from the STEP files.
Fastener designations come from the source illustration; socket drive types, key sizes, and approach directions are heuristic assumptions.
Tool accessibility is checked only in this static arrangement; joint motion, fit, preload, thread engagement, manufacturability, and structural integrity remain unverified.
